title: Scalar upwind update is L-infinity stable under a CFL condition

source.citation: Randall J. LeVeque, Finite Volume Methods for Hyperbolic Problems, Cambridge University Press, 2002, Ch. 4 and Sec. 6.4.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For the scalar upwind update uNext = (1 - cfl) u + cfl uLeft, if 0 <= cfl <= 1 and both |uLeft| and |u| are bounded by M, then |uNext| is also bounded by M.
informal.latex: Let \(\lambda\in\mathbb{R}\) satisfy \(0\le\lambda\le1\). Define \(u^{+}=(1-\lambda)u+\lambda u_L\). If \(|u_L|\le M\) and \(|u|\le M\), then \(|u^{+}|\le M\).
assumptions: cfl, uLeft, u, and M are real numbers
informal.expected_conclusion: The scalar upwind update remains bounded by M in absolute value.

declarations.name: upwindStep
declarations.statement: def upwindStep (cfl uLeft u : ℝ) : ℝ := (1 - cfl) * u + cfl * uLeft

declarations.name: upwindStep_abs_le
declarations.statement: theorem upwindStep_abs_le (cfl uLeft u M : ℝ) (hcfl0 : 0 ≤ cfl) (hcfl1 : cfl ≤ 1) (hLeft : |uLeft| ≤ M) (h : |u| ≤ M) : |upwindStep cfl uLeft u| ≤ M

tasks.input: Translate the scalar upwind CFL L-infinity stability statement into a Lean 4 theorem using upwindStep.
tasks.target: theorem upwindStep_abs_le (cfl uLeft u M : ℝ) (hcfl0 : 0 ≤ cfl) (hcfl1 : cfl ≤ 1) (hLeft : |uLeft| ≤ M) (h : |u| ≤ M) : |upwindStep cfl uLeft u| ≤ M

tasks.input: Given the upwindStep definition and theorem statement, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof using nonnegativity of the convex weights, abs_add_le, abs_mul, mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left, linarith, and ring.

tasks.input: Find the core Mathlib lemmas needed to prove scalar CFL stability of a convex combination.
